title: anthemis stiparum subsp. stiparum
intro: Anthemis stiparum subsp. stiparum, commonly known as the alpine chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis stiparum plant, which belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The alpine chamomile is a low-growing, perennial herb with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The herbal preparations of the alpine chamomile include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to calm and soothe the mind and body.
